title: Jonathan W. Rinzler
content:
{{Top|rwp}} {{Person |image=[[File:Rinzler.png]] |name=Jonathan W. Rinzler |birth=[[August 17]], [[1960s#1962|1962]]{{WebCite|url=https://www.berkeleyside.org/2021/08/04/jonathan-rinzler-obituary-making-of-star-wars-indiana-jones-author|text=Remembering Jonathan Rinzler|author=The Rinzler Family|date=2021-08-04|archivedate=20210804155657}} |death=[[July 28]], [[2021]] |listedpronouns= |nationality=[[Wikipedia:Americans|American]] |occupation=*Author *Executive editor |companies=[[LucasBooks]] |website=[http://www.jwrinzler.com jwrinzler.com] }} {{Quote|I was hired as the nonfiction editor, which, by LucasBooks' strange definition, also included all of DK's ''Star Wars'' books because they are not story driven (but they are).|Jonathan W. Rinzler|{{SW|url=news/introducing-jonathan-rinzler|text=Introducing... Jonathan Rinzler}}}} '''Jonathan W. Rinzler''' was an author, film historian, and executive editor for [[Lucas Licensing]]'s book division, [[LucasBooks]]. ==Biography== In [[2005]], Jonathan W. Rinzler was hired to write ''Star Wars'' books, beginning with two that were published on the [[April 2|same day]],{{SWArchive|url=episode-iii/release/publishing/news20040719.html|text=Del Rey Goes Behind the Scenes of Episode III}} the art book ''[[The Art of Star Wars Episode III: Revenge of the Sith]]''''[[The Art of Star Wars Episode III: Revenge of the Sith]]'' and the real-world reference book ''[[The Making of Star Wars Revenge of the Sith]]'' which offered behind-the-scenes looks at the 2005 [[prequel trilogy]] film, [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']].''[[The Making of Star Wars Revenge of the Sith]]'' He went on to write the ''[[Star Wars Insider]]'' supplements ''[[The Art of Revenge of the Sith]]'' that same year.[[Star Wars Insider 83|''Star Wars Insider'' 83]] In [[2007]], he wrote and published ''[[The Making of Star Wars: The Definitive Story Behind the Original Film]]'' on [[April 24]].''[[The Making of Star Wars: The Definitive Story Behind the Original Film]]'' Rinzler wrote additional ''Star Wars'' reference books in the [[2010]]s, starting with ''[[The Sounds of Star Wars]]''''[[The Sounds of Star Wars]]'' and ''[[The Making of The Empire Strikes Back]]'', both published in 2010.''[[The Making of The Empire Strikes Back]]'' Rinzler wrote and published ''[[Star Wars: The Blueprints]]'' in [[2011]]''[[Star Wars: The Blueprints]]'' and ''[[The Making of Return of the Jedi]]'' in [[2013]].''[[The Making of Return of the Jedi]]'' Rinzler lobbied to be a writer for both ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars]]'' and ''[[Star Wars: Underworld]]'',{{WebCite|url=http://www.rebelforceradio.com/shows/2020/8/21/skywalker-ranch-stories-with-jw-rinzler|text=Skywalker Ranch Stories with J.W. Rinzler|date=2020-08-21|work=Rebel Force Radio|archivedate=20200825234246}} and ended up being credited as writer of the [[The Clone Wars: The Lost Missions|sixth season]] episodes "[[The Disappeared, Part I]]"{{TCW|The Disappeared, Part I}} and "[[The Disappeared, Part II]]."{{TCW|The Disappeared, Part II}} On [[July 28]], [[2021]], Rinzler passed away after a eleven-month battle with pancreatic cancer. ==Works== ===Bibliography===
